note: Auto-generated by the card-first-project-discovery workflow. Unique MISSING projects (not yet in src/content/projects). Each is mapped to the card it anchors. Edit freely, then feed to research-project / batch-cards.

generated_projects.name: Disney Audio-Animatronics (Mr. Lincoln)
generated_projects.role: The first publicly exhibited life-sized humanoid figure whose face, lips, and limbs were synchronized to recorded speech — the origin point of expressive mechanical performance and the lineage all later emotive robots descend from.

generated_projects.name: Disney BDX Droids
generated_projects.role: AI-driven bipedal walking theme-park robots whose locomotion and character emerge from reinforcement learning — the current frontier of expressive autonomous robotic characters in public space.

generated_projects.name: Keepon
generated_projects.role: A minimal two-snowball body with only four degrees of freedom that expresses attention through gaze/posture and emotion through rocking and bouncing — a landmark proof that a creature need not have a face to read as emotionally present.

generated_projects.name: Leonardo (MIT / Stan Winston Studio)
generated_projects.role: A 2.5-foot creature-robot with 61 degrees of freedom — over half in the face — built by MIT's Personal Robots Group and Hollywood animatronics studio Stan Winston, bridging cinematic character performance and social HRI research.

generated_projects.name: Philips iCat
generated_projects.role: A 13-servo desktop cat-robot research platform that produced mapped facial expressions (happy, sad, angry, surprised) via eyebrows, eyelids, and mouth plus LED ears — shipped to labs worldwide and recognized by Time Magazine as one of its Most Amazing Inventions of 2005.

generated_projects.name: Active Badge Location System
generated_projects.role: Badges worn on the body emit IR signals to room-level infrastructure sensors — the earliest deployed system explicitly built around the interplay of on-body and across-room spatial positioning for context-aware computing.

generated_projects.name: Steve Mann's WearComp
generated_projects.role: The first on-body general-purpose computer — a backpack-mounted 6502 system with head-mounted display worn continuously on the body — defining the on-body extreme of the spatial spectrum.

generated_projects.name: Apple Knowledge Navigator
generated_projects.role: A 1987 Apple concept video depicting a bow-tied AI agent on a tablet that schedules, researches, and communicates on command — the foundational design fiction that predicted Siri and GPT-4o by decades.

generated_projects.name: Apple Newton MessagePad
generated_projects.role: The first mass-market PDA — coined the term 'personal digital assistant' and established handwriting-input for contacts, notes, and calendars, even if its recognition was notoriously imperfect.

generated_projects.name: BlackBerry
generated_projects.role: The always-on push-email device that made the executive's inbox portable — its QWERTY thumb keyboard and calendar sync turned the mobile phone into an always-available personal assistant for messaging and scheduling.

generated_projects.name: IBM Simon Personal Communicator
generated_projects.role: The world's first smartphone — combining a cellular phone with PDA features (calendar, address book, email, handwriting) in one device, establishing the convergence that every phone-based assistant since has inherited.

generated_projects.name: Microsoft Cortana
generated_projects.role: Microsoft's voice assistant that bridged phone and desktop Windows — notable for its 'notebook' of learned preferences and quiet hours, and for illustrating how the assistant wars of the mid-2010s played out across every platform.

generated_projects.name: PalmPilot
generated_projects.role: The first commercially successful PDA — pocket-sized, with Graffiti handwriting input and one-button HotSync to your desktop PC, it defined what a practical personal assistant in your pocket looked and felt like.

generated_projects.name: Psion Organiser
generated_projects.role: The world's first practical pocket computer and precursor to all PDAs — a wallet-sized device for contacts, calendars, and notes that established the form factor of the personal digital assistant.

generated_projects.name: Wildfire Communications
generated_projects.role: The first natural-language voice assistant accessed by telephone — users dialed in and spoke commands to a personified AI secretary that managed calls, messages, and contacts, predating Siri by 17 years.

generated_projects.name: Josephine Cochrane's Dishwasher
generated_projects.role: The first patented mechanical dishwashing machine — the original purpose-built appliance to automate a household cleaning chore, replacing hand scrubbing with water-jet pressure.

generated_projects.name: Moley Robotic Kitchen
generated_projects.role: The first robotic kitchen with dexterous chef-arms that learn cooking motions from human chefs and replay them — the highest-expression attempt to fully automate cooking as a physical manipulation task inside the home.

generated_projects.name: Vorwerk Thermomix TM6
generated_projects.role: The connected, guided-cooking iteration of the Thermomix — a networked kitchen appliance that downloads recipes, controls its own temperature and timing step-by-step, and represents the smart-appliance model of AI-assisted domestic cooking.

generated_projects.name: Thermomix VM2000
generated_projects.role: The first heat-and-mix kitchen machine to combine motor-driven chopping, blending, and cooking in a single appliance — founding the multi-function automated cooking category.

generated_projects.name: Cozi Central
generated_projects.role: The first dedicated shared-family digital organizer (launched September 2006), giving every member of a household a common calendar, shopping list, and messaging layer — a software anchor for multi-user domestic coordination that prefigured smart-display family hubs.

generated_projects.name: Nintendo Wii
generated_projects.role: Returned shared physical play to the living room via motion controls — grandparents, children, and guests could all participate in Wii Sports together, making it a landmark case where a consumer device deliberately targeted the full-household social group rather than a solo gamer.

generated_projects.name: DARPA Grand Challenge
generated_projects.role: The 2004–2007 challenges that proved end-to-end autonomous ground vehicles were achievable, shifting the question of trust-and-delegation in driving from science fiction to an engineering race — the direct lineage of today's Waymo and Tesla Autopilot.

generated_projects.name: Sheridan & Verplank Levels of Automation
generated_projects.role: The canonical 10-level taxonomy (notify → recommend → act-with-confirmation → autonomous) that gives this card its conceptual spine; the 1978 MIT supervisory-control paper is the intellectual origin of the card's entire axis.

generated_projects.name: Sperry Gyroscopic Autopilot
generated_projects.role: The first mechanical system to actively control an aircraft without human input — Lawrence Sperry's gyroscope-stabilized autopilot is the historical origin of delegating control authority to a machine, the precursor to every modern autonomy tier.

generated_projects.name: Tesla Autopilot
generated_projects.role: The first mainstream SAE Level 2 driver-assistance system deployed to millions of consumers — popularized the idea of 'conditional delegation' where the car steers and brakes autonomously while the human remains nominally responsible, crystallizing public debate about where trust ends.

generated_projects.name: Ambient Orb
generated_projects.role: The first commercial ambient-information product — a frosted-glass glowing orb from MIT Media Lab spin-off Ambient Devices that maps stock, weather, or traffic data to color, making peripheral data legible without a screen.

generated_projects.name: ambientROOM
generated_projects.role: The MIT Tangible Media Group's first full architectural ambient-display prototype, turning a room's surfaces, light, airflow, and water into a calm information layer.

generated_projects.name: Dangling String (Live Wire)
generated_projects.role: The canonical hardware embodiment of calm technology — a motorized string in a hallway twitches with Ethernet traffic, conveying digital state as peripheral physical motion without screens.

generated_projects.name: Informative Art
generated_projects.role: Holmquist and Skog's research program embedding live data feeds into wall art styled after Mondrian — ambient displays that blend into the decor and surface information only peripherally.

generated_projects.name: Be My Eyes
generated_projects.role: Mobile crowdsourcing app connecting blind users to sighted volunteers (and later AI) via live video for real-time visual assistance — demonstrated a networked, on-demand human+AI assistance model for independent living.

generated_projects.name: Dragon NaturallySpeaking
generated_projects.role: First consumer continuous speech-recognition software, unlocking computer access for people with motor impairments, RSI, and other disabilities — popularized voice as an accessible input modality two decades before smart speakers.

generated_projects.name: DynaVox Speech-Generating Device
generated_projects.role: First touchscreen speech-generating device, giving non-verbal users with cerebral palsy and ALS an independent voice — the foundational commercial AAC device and origin of the Tobii Dynavox lineage.

generated_projects.name: Everest & Jennings Electric Wheelchair
generated_projects.role: First mass-produced electric wheelchair, enabling independent mobility for people with paralysis and severe motor impairment — the foundational physical-independence device of the assistive technology lineage.

generated_projects.name: House/3M Cochlear Implant
generated_projects.role: First FDA-approved cochlear implant, the first device ever approved to replace a human sense — established the category of surgically implanted electronic sensory prosthetics and opened the path to bionic hearing.

generated_projects.name: JAWS Screen Reader
generated_projects.role: Pioneering screen reader enabling blind and visually impaired users to access DOS and later Windows computers via text-to-speech and refreshable Braille — the dominant assistive software platform for decades and a template for digital accessibility.

generated_projects.name: Kurzweil Reading Machine
generated_projects.role: First print-to-speech reading machine for the blind, combining omni-font OCR, flatbed scanning, and text-to-speech — regarded at launch as the most significant advance for the blind since Braille, and the direct ancestor of modern AI visual-description tools.

generated_projects.name: Lifeline Personal Emergency Response System
generated_projects.role: First commercial personal emergency response system (PERS), originating the wearable button-press paradigm for independent living by older adults and people with disabilities — direct ancestor of modern medical alert wearables.

generated_projects.name: Microsoft Seeing AI
generated_projects.role: AI-powered smartphone app that narrates the visual world — reading text, identifying people, objects, currency, and scenes — representing the convergence of on-device deep learning with accessibility for the blind.

generated_projects.name: OrCam MyEye
generated_projects.role: Wearable AI camera that clips to glasses and reads text, identifies faces, and describes products aloud in real time — brought portable, on-body computer vision to the blind and low-vision community as a standalone consumer product.

generated_projects.name: ReWalk Exoskeleton
generated_projects.role: First FDA-approved powered lower-limb exoskeleton for home and community use by paraplegics, establishing the category of wearable robotic rehabilitation devices for spinal cord injury.

generated_projects.name: Tobii Eye Tracker (MyTobii D10)
generated_projects.role: First desktop eye-gaze communication aid, enabling people with ALS and severe motor disabilities to control a computer and speak using only eye movement — bridged eye-tracking research into a clinical AAC product category.

generated_projects.name: Active Badge Location System
generated_projects.role: The foundational context-aware system: infrared-emitting badges tracked occupant location room-by-room across an office network, enabling the first identity+location-aware call routing and directory services.

generated_projects.name: Apple Intelligence (Personal Context System)
generated_projects.role: State-of-the-art on-device personal context system: combines a ~3B-parameter on-device LLM with calendar, email, photos, and on-screen awareness so Siri can act on the full personal context of one user — with privacy-preserving Private Cloud Compute for heavier tasks.

generated_projects.name: AWARE Mobile Context Instrumentation Framework
generated_projects.role: Open-source Android/iOS research platform that continuously streams sensor, location, and behavioral data for context-aware app development, enabling reproducible academic studies at scale.

generated_projects.name: Aware Home Research Initiative — Georgia Tech
generated_projects.role: A full-scale instrumented house used as a living laboratory for pervasive sensing research; pioneered domestic context-awareness covering occupant activity, health, and environment since 2000.

generated_projects.name: Context Toolkit (Dey & Abowd)
generated_projects.role: Provided the first reusable middleware abstraction for context-aware applications — widgets, aggregators, and interpreters — establishing the software architecture vocabulary still referenced today.

generated_projects.name: Cyberguide — Mobile Context-Aware Tour Guide
generated_projects.role: Georgia Tech's handheld outdoor tour guide used location and orientation as context to deliver place-specific information; one of the first field-deployed context-aware mobile apps.

generated_projects.name: Foursquare
generated_projects.role: Brought location-aware context to mass consumer smartphones via GPS check-ins: popularized the idea that place and time constitute actionable personal context, and later became a B2B location intelligence platform.

generated_projects.name: Google Now
generated_projects.role: First mainstream proactive context-aware assistant: fused location, calendar, search history, and time-of-day to deliver unrequested but useful 'cards' before the user asked — defining the ambient push model of context-aware computing.

generated_projects.name: GUIDE — Context-Aware City Visitor Guide (Lancaster)
generated_projects.role: Lancaster University's GUIDE deployed a wireless handheld that delivered tailored city information to visitors based on their location, personal profile, and time — an early field trial merging adaptive hypermedia with context sensing.

generated_projects.name: PARCTAB Ubiquitous Computing Experiment
generated_projects.role: First deployed context-aware PDA: palm-sized wireless tablets that used IR room-cell location to filter files, control devices, and adapt UI to the user's physical position inside Xerox PARC.

generated_projects.name: PlaceLab — Open-Source Location-Aware Computing Toolkit
generated_projects.role: Intel Research's open toolkit used Wi-Fi and GSM beacon fingerprinting to provide low-cost, privacy-preserving location for any device — bridging lab systems to real-world urban deployment at scale.

generated_projects.name: Schilit & Theimer — Disseminating Active Map Information
generated_projects.role: Coined the term 'context-aware computing' and defined its four application types (proximate selection, auto-reconfiguration, contextual commands, context-triggered actions) — the conceptual DNA of the entire field.

generated_projects.name: HAL2000 (Home Automated Living)
generated_projects.role: A Windows 95 software package from 1997 that added voice control to X10 devices — predating Alexa by nearly two decades, letting users say 'turn off the lights' and receive spoken traffic reports, stock quotes, and news from a PC connected to their powerline network.

generated_projects.name: NAHB Smart House Project
generated_projects.role: The industry consortium that coined the term 'Smart House' in 1984 and built a shared R&D program to integrate gas, electric, and data wiring into a single coordinated domestic network — the first organized attempt to define what an intelligent home should be.

generated_projects.name: X10 (home automation protocol)
generated_projects.role: The first general-purpose home automation protocol: powerline signaling that let a command console switch lights and appliances in any room, originating in Scotland in 1975 and reaching RadioShack shelves in 1978.

generated_projects.name: Androbot TOPO
generated_projects.role: Nolan Bushnell's consumer home robot debuted commercially in 1983, sold as an Apple II-controlled domestic companion — one of the first to attract mass media attention as a product for ordinary homes.

generated_projects.name: Electrolux Trilobite
generated_projects.role: The world's first commercially available robot vacuum, predating the Roomba by a year — proved autonomous room-mapping and docking in a consumer product.

generated_projects.name: RB5X
generated_projects.role: First mass-produced programmable personal robot marketed directly to home users — the earliest confirmed commercial product in this lineage.

generated_projects.name: Wakamaru
generated_projects.role: Mitsubishi's yellow companion robot, test-sold to 100 Japanese households in 2005 — the earliest documented case of a full-body social robot commercially deployed inside ordinary homes.

generated_projects.name: Agility Robotics Digit
generated_projects.role: First humanoid robot deployed commercially in logistics warehouses (Ford, then Amazon) — bridged the gap between research platform and paying industrial customer.

generated_projects.name: Atlas (Boston Dynamics, 2013)
generated_projects.role: DARPA-funded hydraulic humanoid built for disaster response — redefined expectations for bipedal agility and became the benchmark robot for research teams worldwide.

generated_projects.name: Boston Dynamics Atlas (Electric, 2024)
generated_projects.role: All-electric successor to the hydraulic Atlas — superhuman range of motion, designed for industrial deployment with Hyundai, closing the loop from DARPA research to production.

generated_projects.name: ASIMO
generated_projects.role: Honda's child-sized humanoid (2000–2022) that could walk, run, climb stairs, recognise faces, and pour drinks — the defining public symbol of humanoid robots for two decades.

generated_projects.name: Honda E0 / E-series
generated_projects.role: Honda's secret first bipedal walkers (E0–E6, 1986–1993): slow-step static locomotion studies that laid the kinematic groundwork for P2 and ultimately ASIMO.

generated_projects.name: Honda P2
generated_projects.role: First fully self-contained, self-regulating bipedal humanoid — autonomous stair-climbing and obstacle avoidance without an external computer or power tether, an IEEE Milestone.

generated_projects.name: HRP-2
generated_projects.role: Japan's METI-backed open research humanoid (Kawada / AIST) — the shared platform that powered a generation of manipulation, lying-down-and-getting-up, and whole-body motion research.

generated_projects.name: Sophia (Hanson Robotics)
generated_projects.role: Hyper-realistic social humanoid with 60+ facial expressions and AI conversation — a cultural inflection point that brought humanoid robots into mainstream media debate.

generated_projects.name: WABOT-1
generated_projects.role: World's first full-scale humanoid robot: bipedal walking, rudimentary hands, Japanese speech, and artificial vision — defined the research agenda for the entire field.

generated_projects.name: WABOT-2
generated_projects.role: Specialist humanoid that could read sheet music and play an electronic organ with two hands and two feet — demonstrated purposeful dexterous manipulation well before ASIMO-era robots.

generated_projects.name: ASEA IRB 6
generated_projects.role: The world's first fully electric, microprocessor-controlled commercial industrial robot (ASEA/ABB, Sweden, 1974), marking the shift from hydraulic to electric drive that all modern arms follow.

generated_projects.name: Franka Emika Panda
generated_projects.role: A 7-DOF torque-controlled research arm (2017) that became the de-facto standard platform for AI/ML manipulation research; its open control interface and 1 kHz torque sensing made it ubiquitous in deep-learning-for-robotics labs.

generated_projects.name: KUKA LBR iiwa
generated_projects.role: The first series-produced robot with torque sensing in all 7 joints (2013); enabled compliant, force-sensitive manipulation and became the dominant research arm for learning-based manipulation through the 2010s.

generated_projects.name: Physical Intelligence π0
generated_projects.role: A generalist robot foundation model (Oct 2024) trained across 7 platforms and 68 tasks; the first demonstration that a single VLA policy can perform laundry folding, box assembly, and bussing — marking the shift from single-task to general manipulation intelligence.

generated_projects.name: PUMA (Programmable Universal Machine for Assembly)
generated_projects.role: Unimation/GM's commercially produced 6-DOF arm (1978), derived from the Stanford Arm; became the canonical research and teaching platform for two decades and the first to have a dedicated programming language (VAL).

generated_projects.name: Rethink Robotics Baxter
generated_projects.role: Rodney Brooks's dual-arm cobot (2012) that could be trained by physically guiding its arms; brought human-safe, low-cost ($22k) manipulation to factory floors and demonstrated behavioral programming without code.

generated_projects.name: Shakey the Robot
generated_projects.role: SRI's first AI-driven mobile robot (1966–72); while primarily a navigation platform, Shakey used pushing to manipulate objects and pioneered the integration of planning and physical action that underpins all intelligent manipulation.

generated_projects.name: Stanford Arm
generated_projects.role: Victor Scheinman's 6-DOF all-electric manipulator at Stanford AI Lab — the first robot arm designed purely for computer control, directly enabling closed-form kinematics and leading to the PUMA series.

generated_projects.name: Unimate
generated_projects.role: The first industrial robot arm deployed on a production line (GM, 1961), invented by George Devol and commercialized with Joseph Engelberger — the direct origin of the manipulation-bot lineage.

generated_projects.name: Universal Robots UR5
generated_projects.role: The first commercially viable collaborative robot (cobot), shipped in 2008; eliminated safety caging by making the arm safe enough to work alongside humans, democratizing manipulation for small manufacturers.

generated_projects.name: Willow Garage PR2
generated_projects.role: The first open-platform mobile manipulator (2010) with dual 7-DOF arms on an omnidirectional base; spawned ROS and an entire research ecosystem, making household manipulation a credible research agenda.

generated_projects.name: NAO
generated_projects.role: The standard HRI research platform for a decade — Aldebaran's affordable, programmable humanoid became the robot used in more social robotics studies than any other, normalizing the field's methods and curriculum.

generated_projects.name: Grey Walter's Tortoises (Elmer & Elsie)
generated_projects.role: The first autonomous robots with reactive, light-seeking behavior — widely cited as the founding moment of behavior-based robotics and an acknowledged precursor to social robotics.

generated_projects.name: MIT Cog
generated_projects.role: Rodney Brooks's humanoid upper-torso robot at MIT, the direct research platform from which Kismet and the field of social robotics grew; demonstrated face tracking, joint attention, and human-style physical interaction.

generated_projects.name: MIT Leonardo
generated_projects.role: Breazeal's follow-on to Kismet — a 60-motor expressive social robot that could learn by watching and imitating humans, advancing joint attention and collaborative task work as HRI research concerns.

generated_projects.name: Tamagotchi
generated_projects.role: First mass-market digital companion — a handheld egg that required ongoing emotional care, proving millions of people would form attachment bonds with artificial creatures and establishing the 'always-on' companion paradigm.

generated_projects.name: ARToolKit
generated_projects.role: Hirokazu Kato's open-source marker-tracking library put real-time AR registration in researchers' hands for the first time, seeding the developer ecosystem that later produced mobile and web AR.

generated_projects.name: CAVE (Cave Automatic Virtual Environment)
generated_projects.role: Cruz-Neira, Sandin, and DeFanti's room-sized projection system at EVL/UIC pioneered room-scale immersive VR without a headset, establishing spatial computing as an architectural / multi-user form factor.

generated_projects.name: Google Glass
generated_projects.role: The first widely-distributed consumer AR monocle brought spatial overlays (navigation, photos, voice search) to everyday eyewear, triggering the first mainstream cultural debate about always-on head-worn cameras.

generated_projects.name: Microsoft HoloLens
generated_projects.role: The first self-contained holographic mixed-reality headset with inside-out spatial mapping and hand/gaze interaction, establishing the untethered MR platform that industry and enterprise spatial computing is built on.

generated_projects.name: Milgram Reality–Virtuality Continuum
generated_projects.role: Paul Milgram and Fumio Kishino's taxonomy paper coined 'mixed reality' and defined the AR–VR spectrum, giving the field its shared vocabulary and the conceptual frame that all modern headsets are designed within.

generated_projects.name: NASA VIEW System
generated_projects.role: NASA Ames' Virtual Interface Environment Workstation coupled HMD, 3D audio, speech recognition, and data gloves for telerobotics — the first government-backed integrated spatial computing platform.

generated_projects.name: Oculus Rift
generated_projects.role: Palmer Luckey's Kickstarter prototype and 2016 consumer launch reignited the VR industry after a decade of dormancy, making low-latency 6-DOF head-tracking affordable and seeding the current headset generation.

generated_projects.name: Pokemon Go
generated_projects.role: Niantic's location-based AR game put spatial computing in 500 million pockets overnight — the first mass demonstration that overlaying the digital on the physical world could drive millions of people outdoors.

generated_projects.name: Sensorama
generated_projects.role: Morton Heilig's multi-sensory arcade simulator — stereoscopic film, spatial sound, vibration, scent — is the earliest physical prototype of immersive, body-engaged virtual experience before digital computation.

generated_projects.name: Sutherland's Sword of Damocles
generated_projects.role: The first head-mounted 3D display with real-time head tracking, establishing the core HMD paradigm — stereoscopic display plus positional sensing — that every spatial headset descends from.

generated_projects.name: Sutherland's Ultimate Display
generated_projects.role: Sutherland's 1965 IFIP paper coined the vision of a room where the computer controls the existence of matter — the philosophical origin of spatial computing as a discipline.

generated_projects.name: Videoplace
generated_projects.role: Myron Krueger's camera-based responsive environment created a shared 'artificial reality' through full-body silhouette interaction without goggles or gloves, pioneering controller-free spatial interaction.

generated_projects.name: VPL Research EyePhone / DataGlove
generated_projects.role: Jaron Lanier's VPL commercialized the first consumer VR glove (DataGlove, 1987) and HMD (EyePhone), establishing 'virtual reality' as a term and the glove-plus-headset body interface still referenced today.

generated_projects.name: ADT Safe-Watch / Central Station Monitoring
generated_projects.role: ADT launched the first electronic 24/7 central monitoring station in the mid-1970s; its Safewatch residential surveillance product line debuted in the early 1980s — bridging the gap between industrial CCTV and the smart-home era by bringing professional continuous monitoring to middle-income households.

generated_projects.name: Arlo Wire-Free HD Security Camera
generated_projects.role: Launched by Netgear in November 2014 as the first truly wire-free (battery + wireless) HD home security camera, Arlo removed the last installation barrier and accelerated the shift to DIY ambient home surveillance.

generated_projects.name: Axis NetEye 200 (First IP Network Camera)
generated_projects.role: The world's first network-connected camera (1 frame/17 seconds), launched by Axis Communications in 1996, moved surveillance from closed coaxial loops to open IP networks — the technical pivot that eventually made cloud-streamed home cameras possible.

generated_projects.name: Siemens / Walter Bruch CCTV (V-2 Launch Monitor)
generated_projects.role: The first documented closed-circuit television system, installed at Peenemünde by Siemens AG under engineer Walter Bruch to monitor V-2 rocket launches from a safe distance — the technical origin of all CCTV surveillance lineage.

generated_projects.name: Dropcam
generated_projects.role: Founded in 2009 and shipping cloud-streaming Wi-Fi cameras from 2011, Dropcam was the first widely accessible consumer device to put continuous home video in the cloud — directly seeding the modern smart-home camera category before its $555M acquisition by Nest in 2014.

generated_projects.name: Marie Van Brittan Brown Home Security System
generated_projects.role: The first patented residential television-surveillance system — four peepholes, a sliding camera, a monitor, two-way audio, and a remote door latch — establishing the conceptual blueprint for video doorbells and home-care cameras decades before the technology was affordable.

generated_projects.name: Nest Cam IQ
generated_projects.role: The first consumer home camera to ship with on-device facial recognition (via Google AI / Nest Aware), tracking subjects in 4K and alerting owners to strangers vs. known faces — a milestone in AI-powered domestic surveillance.

generated_projects.name: Ring Familiar Faces (AI Facial Recognition)
generated_projects.role: Rolled out in December 2025, Ring's opt-in Familiar Faces feature uses on-device facial recognition to name visitors at the door ('Mom at Front Door'), representing the state-of-art in AI-powered residential surveillance and the sharpest privacy flashpoint in consumer home cameras.

generated_projects.name: Sentinare AI Fall-Detection Camera
generated_projects.role: An AI camera purpose-built for elder-care fall detection by AltumView (Vancouver, Canada), using computer vision to recognize falls and alert caregivers in near-real-time without requiring wearables — representing the care-camera branch of the genealogy reaching toward clinical-grade passive monitoring.

generated_projects.name: Wyze Cam
generated_projects.role: Launched at $19.99 in October 2017 by ex-Amazon engineers, Wyze Cam democratized AI home surveillance — person, pet, and package detection with no mandatory subscription — making intelligent home cameras accessible to the mass market rather than only premium buyers.

generated_projects.name: Zenith Radio Nurse
generated_projects.role: The world's first electronic baby monitor — a two-part audio surveillance device designed by Isamu Noguchi for Zenith Radio Corporation — establishes the foundational impulse to make domestic life legible to a remote listener, the direct conceptual ancestor of home-care cameras.

generated_projects.name: Active Badge (Olivetti Research Laboratory)
generated_projects.role: The first practical indoor location system — IR-emitting badges worn by staff, detected by a room sensor network — demonstrating that context (specifically, location) could be woven invisibly into the office environment.

generated_projects.name: Auto-ID Center / Electronic Product Code (EPC)
generated_projects.role: The MIT consortium founded in 1999 that coined 'Internet of Things', developed the EPC standard for low-cost RFID tagging, and supplied the supply-chain infrastructure that mass-deployed Weiser's vision of uniquely identified physical objects networked globally.

generated_projects.name: Dangling String (Calm Technology ambient display)
generated_projects.role: Artist Natalie Jeremijenko's installation at Xerox PARC — a plastic string twitching with Ethernet traffic — is the canonical demonstration of calm technology: network data rendered as peripheral, ambient sensation with no screen.

generated_projects.name: Georgia Tech Aware Home
generated_projects.role: A full-scale research home built at Georgia Tech in 1998 as the first residential laboratory of its kind — embedding sensors, context-aware computing, and activity recognition into a real domestic floor plan to study how a home could perceive and assist its occupants.

generated_projects.name: HP Labs Cooltown
generated_projects.role: HP Research's Cooltown project gave every physical person, place, and thing a Web presence via URL beaconing, materializing the ubicomp goal of seamlessly linking physical and digital worlds through internet-standard infrastructure.

generated_projects.name: MIT Project Oxygen
generated_projects.role: MIT CSAIL's five-year pervasive computing initiative that prototyped human-centered, anywhere/anytime computing through natural speech and vision interfaces, bridging ubicomp theory to deployable systems across a corporate-backed alliance.

generated_projects.name: Smart Dust (UC Berkeley)
generated_projects.role: Kris Pister's millimeter-scale wireless sensor motes — the extreme miniaturization end of ubicomp — demonstrated that sensing could be dispersed invisibly into environments at scale, directly instantiating Weiser's promise of invisible computation.

generated_projects.name: Tangible Bits
generated_projects.role: Ishii and Ullmer's CHI '97 paper founding Tangible User Interfaces — coupling bits to physical objects and ambient media — is the intellectual bridge between ubicomp and embodied, spatially situated interaction design.

generated_projects.name: Context Toolkit (Abowd / Dey, Georgia Tech)
generated_projects.role: The first reusable software infrastructure for building context-aware applications, introducing 'context widgets' that abstract raw sensor data the same way GUI widgets abstract user input — making context a programmable building block rather than an ad-hoc hack.

generated_projects.name: EasyLiving (Microsoft Research)
generated_projects.role: A smart-room prototype that used multi-camera person tracking to maintain a live world-model of who was where, routing device control (lights, displays) to the right person automatically — one of the first systems to treat people's physical location as the primary context signal for environment control.

generated_projects.name: Gemini Live (Google)
generated_projects.role: Represents the current frontier of real-time multimodal context streaming — a model that ingests live video, audio, and text simultaneously with sub-second latency, letting AI reason about and respond to a dynamic physical environment as it evolves second-by-second.

generated_projects.name: MavHome (Managing an Adaptive Versatile Home)
generated_projects.role: An agent-based smart home that applied machine learning to predict inhabitants' next actions and device usage from a stream of sensor events — one of the first systems to treat context not just as a state to detect but as a sequence to predict and act on.

generated_projects.name: RADAR (Microsoft Research)
generated_projects.role: The first Wi-Fi signal-strength indoor positioning system, demonstrating that commodity wireless infrastructure already present in buildings could locate people to within a few meters — an enabling layer that made fine-grained location a practical input for context-aware services.

generated_projects.name: Schilit & Theimer Context-Aware Computing / ParcTab
generated_projects.role: Coined the term 'context-aware computing' and demonstrated the first four categories of context-adaptive applications on the ParcTab handheld — proximate selection, auto-reconfiguration, contextual commands, and context-triggered actions — making it the conceptual ground-zero of the field.

generated_projects.name: ChatGPT Memory
generated_projects.role: OpenAI's cross-session persistent memory feature for ChatGPT, which saves user facts and preferences across conversations — the first mainstream LLM to offer opt-in autobiographical memory at scale, transforming a stateless assistant into a remembering one.

generated_projects.name: CYRUS — Case-Based Reasoning System
generated_projects.role: The first AI program to model episodic storage and retrieval from a personal memory, built by Janet Kolodner at Yale — answering questions about Secretary of State Cyrus Vance's diplomatic calendar using hierarchical episodic memory packets, establishing the foundational architecture for AI that remembers individual events.

generated_projects.name: MemGPT
generated_projects.role: A UC Berkeley research system that applied OS-style virtual memory paging to LLMs, giving AI agents a two-tier memory hierarchy (in-context fast memory + external disk storage) and enabling indefinitely long conversations and persistent persona — foundational paper for LLM long-term memory.

generated_projects.name: Microsoft Recall
generated_projects.role: An OS-level AI feature that takes continuous screenshots of everything on a Copilot+ PC and indexes them as a natural-language-searchable 'photographic memory,' making total-recall of digital life a default system capability rather than a separate app.

generated_projects.name: Microsoft SenseCam
generated_projects.role: A neck-worn passive camera that triggered on sensor changes to capture a fisheye snapshot every few minutes, accumulating thousands of images per day as a wearable episodic memory aid — the hardware backbone of the MyLifeBits lifelog.

generated_projects.name: MyLifeBits
generated_projects.role: Gordon Bell's decade-long Microsoft Research experiment to digitize and store every document, phone call, image, and biosensor reading of one person's life — the first systematic attempt at a searchable total-recall autobiographical memory.

generated_projects.name: Narrative Clip
generated_projects.role: The first mass-market consumer lifelogging camera — a clip-on device that automatically photographed life every 30 seconds and synced images to the cloud, bringing passive episodic visual memory capture out of the lab and into everyday life.

generated_projects.name: Rewind AI
generated_projects.role: A macOS app that records everything you see, say, and hear on your computer and makes it locally searchable by natural language — bringing always-on digital memory to the desktop and foreshadowing the Limitless Pendant hardware pivot.

generated_projects.name: Steve Mann — Wearable Wireless Webcam
generated_projects.role: The first documented lifelogging experiment — Mann streamed continuous wearable video to the World Wide Web from 1994–96, coining the term 'lifelogging' and creating the first running first-person visual memory archive.

generated_projects.name: Vannevar Bush — Memex
generated_projects.role: The foundational thought experiment describing a personal memory-extension device that stores all records and lets you traverse them via associative trails — the conceptual origin of every lifelogging and AI memory system that followed.

generated_projects.name: Apple Differential Privacy (iOS 10)
generated_projects.role: Apple's public embrace of differential privacy in 2016 as a counter-design philosophy — learning aggregate patterns without identifying individuals — introduced the on-device vs. cloud processing axis as a competitive privacy differentiator.

generated_projects.name: Apple Private Cloud Compute
generated_projects.role: Apple's architectural answer to cloud AI privacy — processing on hardened Apple Silicon servers with cryptographic attestation and no data retention, setting a new design standard for how ambient AI can be both capable and verifiably private.

generated_projects.name: DoubleClick Cookie Tracking
generated_projects.role: The first mass consumer data-harvesting scandal — cross-site tracking cookies harvesting behavioral profiles without consent — the foundational internet privacy debate that all later smart-home and ambient-AI controversies inherit.

generated_projects.name: Steve Mann WearCam / ShootingBack
generated_projects.role: The earliest wearable always-on camera, deliberately inverting surveillance ('sousveillance') and raising bystander-consent questions decades before Glass or Meta; the conceptual origin of the wearable-camera privacy debate.

generated_projects.name: AutoGPT
generated_projects.role: The first widely viral autonomous LLM agent: given a single goal, it broke tasks into sub-goals and called tools (web search, file I/O, code execution) in a loop without further human prompts — made agentic looping legible to a mass audience.

generated_projects.name: Claude Computer Use
generated_projects.role: Anthropic's October 2024 capability enabling Claude to control a full desktop — clicking, typing, and navigating apps — representing the broadest current scope of LLM-driven tool calls: any software interface becomes a callable tool.

generated_projects.name: General Magic Telescript
generated_projects.role: The first commercial mobile-agent platform: software agents carrying 'work orders' traveled across network nodes to execute tasks (book tickets, retrieve data) and return results — an explicit pre-web tool-call architecture.

generated_projects.name: Microsoft Office Assistant (Clippy)
generated_projects.role: First mainstream consumer-facing proactive agent that watched user actions and offered to invoke document-level tools on their behalf — a Bayesian intent detector triggering scripted actions, prefiguring intent→tool routing in later voice assistants.

generated_projects.name: OpenAI Function Calling API
generated_projects.role: The June 2023 API feature that standardized how LLMs express tool calls as structured JSON — the platform primitive that enabled every subsequent LLM agent framework and made tool-calling a commodity developer interface.

generated_projects.name: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
generated_projects.role: The foundational research paper (Yao et al., Google, 2022) that formalized interleaved reasoning traces and tool-call actions in LLMs — the ReAct loop pattern now underlies virtually every production LLM agent framework.

generated_projects.name: STRIPS / Shakey the Robot
generated_projects.role: The first AI planning system that decomposed a high-level goal into an ordered sequence of discrete actions (tool calls), executed by a physical robot — the deepest origin of agentic action selection.

generated_projects.name: Toolformer
generated_projects.role: First language model trained in a self-supervised way to autonomously decide which APIs to call, when, and with what arguments — the research paper that introduced tool-calling as a learnable LLM capability rather than a hard-coded skill.

generated_projects.name: Yahoo! Pipes
generated_projects.role: First visual web-service composition tool: users wired RSS feeds and web APIs together with drag-and-drop modules to create custom data flows — an early no-code 'agentic workflow' builder predating IFTTT and Zapier.

generated_projects.name: Zapier
generated_projects.role: The developer/power-user counterpart to IFTTT: multi-step 'Zaps' chain API calls across thousands of services, making deterministic, human-authored agentic workflows the standard integration layer for business software.
